session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 9013 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill with the --pr 9013 argument:

</details>", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-08 07:23:25 PT

Found 0 violations across 10 files. All code changes comply with erk coding standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- **Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** → loaded [CLI Error Handling Anti-Patterns](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/blob/master/docs/learned/cli/error-handling-antipatterns.md)

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches
✅ All pattern-based tripwires - None triggered

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches
✅ **Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** - Code correctly migrates FROM Ensure.invariant TO UserFacingCliError (compliant)
✅ **Adding new CLI flags without validation** - JSON flag properly implemented with decorator pattern
✅ **Testing patterns** - Comprehensive test coverage added

### Violations Summary
None found. All changes follow erk conventions:
- ✅ Proper migration from Ensure.invariant to UserFacingCliError for user-facing validation
- ✅ Discriminated unions used correctly (OneShotDispatchResult | OneShotDryRunResult vs T | None)
- ✅ Frozen dataclass patterns maintained
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage for new functionality
- ✅ Error handling with structured error_type field

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/ensure.py`: 0 violations
- `src/erk/cli/json_output.py`: 0 violations (new file)
- `src/erk/core/workflow_smoke_test.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_dispatch.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_json.py`: 0 violations (new file)
- `tests/commands/one_shot/test_one_shot_remote_dispatch.py`: 0 violations
- `tests/unit/cli/test_json_output.py`: 0 violations (new file)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-08 07:23:25 PT**: No violations detected. Code correctly follows CLI error handling patterns (Ensure.invariant → UserFacingCliError migration), uses discriminated unions properly, and includes comprehensive tests.

</details>",

session.assistant:
## Summary
I've successfully executed the pr-feedback-classifier skill for PR #9013. Here's the analysis:
**PR #9013 - Add JSON output support to one-shot command with structured error handling**
The PR has **1 actionable issue** and **6 informational comments**:
### Actionable Issue (1) - **Type**: Dignified Python violation (default parameter) - **Location**: `src/erk/cli/ensure.py:46` - **Issue**: Default parameter value on `error_type` parameter violates erk's LBYL standards (which forbid default parameter values) - **Complexity**: Local (single line fix) - **Auto-proceed**: true
### Informational Comments (6) All informational discussions are status updates and metadata comments: 1. Plan queued for implementation (schrockn) 2. GitHub Action started notification 3. Dignified Python review (1 violation noted) 4. Dignified Code Simplifier review (passed) 5. Test Coverage review (passed) 6. Tripwires review (passed)
### Final JSON Output
```json { "success": true, "pr_number": 9013, "pr_title": "Add JSON output support to one-shot command with structured error handling", "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/9013", "actionable_threads": [ { "thread_id": "dignified-python-violation-1", "type": "discussion", "path": "src/erk/cli/ensure.py", "line": 46, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": false, "action_summary": "Fix default parameter value on error_type parameter (dignified-python violation)", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "Default parameter value on error_type parameter" } ], "discussion_actions": [], "informational_count": 6, "batches": [ { "name": "Local Fixes", "complexity": "local", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [0] }, { "name": "Single-File", "complexity": "single_file", "auto_proceed": true, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Cross-Cutting", "complexity": "cross_cutting", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Complex", "complexity": "complex", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] }, { "name": "Informational", "complexity": "informational", "auto_proceed": false, "item_indices": [] } ], "error": null } ```
